Central intelligence Live demo

One command center. Six specialists. Your business on autopilot — governed.

This is what your buyer sees: a high-intelligence agentic team orbiting their operations — outreach filling pipeline, proof closing demos, builds shipping, guard blocking risk, revenue compounding. Not slides. A live command center.

  • Brain orchestrates from your disk — routes work, never loses context

The problem you already feel

Your agents ran. Your audit trail did not.

You can see the output, but not the decision. SourceA puts a gate in front of every agent move — policy before dispatch, a signed receipt after, and a replay your client can audit on site.

Most teams can show a demo. Few can show what executed at 2am, what was blocked, and what policy version applied — without opening chat history or log spaghetti.

Read the full story →

Closeout has no receipt

Your client asks "what ran?" and you forward logs. They wanted a signed PASS or BLOCK — something counsel can file.

Policy arrives too late

Governance at session start misses the dangerous action at minute forty. You need a gate on every dispatch, not a reminder at kickoff.

Automation without accountability

Workflow tools connect steps. They do not prove outcomes, catch tampering, or let you replay yesterday cold.

01

We run it first

Our own factory runs these loops daily — you get the spine we dogfood, not a slideware promise.

02

Policy before the model

Every action passes your rules on disk before the LLM executes — scoped, feasible, recorded.

03

Receipts your client keeps

Weekly export — HTML or PDF — with what ran, what blocked, and which policy applied.

04

Proof in one sitting

We screen-share ALLOW, BLOCK, and tamper detection in under five minutes. That is the sales call.

How your loop stays governed

Dispatch

Each proposed action is checked against your policy — every time, not once at login.

Sub-millisecond checks

Receipt

ALLOW or BLOCK lands on disk with context — so Monday starts from evidence, not memory.

Synced with your roster

Replay

Re-run last night's turn without chat history. If someone altered a record, you will know.

Demo-ready in minutes

Agentic growth system

Governance is the engine. Revenue is the outcome.

Proof is not paperwork — it is how agencies and platform teams close $3–10K builds in weeks. Outreach books demos. Demos screen-share receipts. Builds become retainers. Every step governed, every step counted.

Turn 01 · LIVE

2:14am — the orchestrator wakes the council. Your policy speaks before your models do.

What you show the buyer

One chain. Six beats. Five minutes.

Skip the deck. Walk from request to receipt to replay — the same path we run live on this sample site, ready for your screen-share.

Step through the proof chain →

request→ policy→ decision→ receipt→ replay→ tamper-FAIL
01

Brief

Scope the job and lock your rules.

02

Dispatch

Policy checked before any agent acts.

03

Run

Specialists execute under the gate.

04

Gate

The gate refuses a bad move — BLOCK.

05

Receipt

Signed evidence written to disk.

06

Replay

Client audits the whole run on site.

ALLOW◆ BLOCK◆ tamper-FAIL
01

Something proposes action

Outreach, ops, research — your loop asks to run. The request enters the governed path.

scoped request → role check
02

Your rules load first

Policy on disk, scope check, feasibility — checked before any model token spends.

rules check → PASS
03

Dispatch gate fires

Not "we talked about governance at kickoff." This action, this moment, this verdict.

04

Verdict lands on disk

ALLOW or BLOCK — recorded where replay can find it tomorrow morning.

PASS · BLOCK · tamper-FAIL
05

The loop runs — or stops

Blocked actions never touch production. Approved ones execute under monitor.

06

You export proof

Weekly bundle for your client: HTML, PDF, replay script, handoff note.

export → replay → handoff
replay · sourcea-boot

Sample boot run · 2026-06-18 00:04:50 UTC · BLOCK

$ sourcea-boot --json
SOURCEA_SAMPLE_BOOT BLOCK ok=false
  [PASS] policy_pack: agency v3.2 current
  [PASS] provider: voyage semantic active
  [PASS] receipt_fresh: receipt fresh (0.3h)
  [FAIL] pipeline_sync: queue mismatch (sample)
REPORT=/Users/sinakazemnezhad/Desktop/SourceA/BOOT_REPORT.json▋

Capability map (illustrative)

Others sell a piece. You need the whole proof.

Builders wire workflows. Orchestrators move tasks. Slides promise governance. You need build, policy, receipt, replay, delivery, and weekly proof — in one row.

Illustrative capability map — not a competitive audit. See full compare for methodology footnote.

Your stack underneath. Our receipts on top.

Cursor WorkerTemporalLangGraphn8nsourcea-bootGitHub evalWeekly PDFSelf-hosted
◇Start in shadowWatch policy fire without touching production — prove value before you enforce.
◇Evidence, not chatReceipts live on your disk — not in a thread someone can misremember.
◇Fail closedWhen policy says BLOCK, the loop stops. Altered receipts surface as tamper-FAIL.
◇Yours to keepSelf-hosted policy law — replay without renting your audit trail from a vendor.

What it costs

Fixed outcomes. No surprise hours.

You are buying a governed loop and the proof that ships with it — not a retainer that invoices for "alignment sessions."

Compare plans and terms →

Ops Health Audit

$750 one-time

  • 48-hour spine review
  • Board-ready PDF report
  • Clear path to a full build
Book audit
Most teams start here

Agent Loop Build

$3–10K project

  • One governed loop live in 2–3 weeks
  • Handoff doc + live replay demo
  • 30-day fix window included

50% to start · balance when your loop passes on disk

Start build

Retainer

$2–5K / month

  • Weekly proof export for clients
  • Changes gated by your approval
  • Self-healing monitor on the loop
